gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 high-reasoning review found one P2: strict benchmark plans accepted a single explicit launch-config sidecar and deferred backend completeness to release_bundle. Fixed by requiring the required number of distinct vLLM/SGLang sidecar paths in strict planning and adding a regression test; delta review found no blocking issues.
verification: PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_benchmark_plan_executor.py::test_execute_benchmark_job_plan_accepts_generated_benchmark_plan_record -q; PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_benchmark_plan.py::test_benchmark_plan_rejects_incomplete_strict_release_bundle tests/test_benchmark_plan.py::test_main_can_include_release_bundle_command -q; PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q (1375 passed); python -m compileall -q src tests; git diff --check
what_changed: Benchmark plans can generate vLLM/SGLang engine launch-config sidecars through --engine-launch-config-output-dir, include generated paths in strict release bundle records/commands, and execute those generated-plan records through the benchmark-plan executor schema.
why: Strict release bundle planning should not depend on hand-written vLLM/SGLang launch JSON now that the package owns validated launch-config builders; the planner should also reject incomplete launch-config sidecar sets before emitting strict plans.
